patches.find: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.
patches.replace: There is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-016 appears only as a one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent. The sole verification is the dome author asserting the calculation matches.
patches.rationale: WIN-016 has no live fetch call (unlike other WINs that do). The boilerplate sentence was copy-pasted from a different WIN and is factually wrong for WIN-016. Curmudgeon c5 H1 flagged this as moderate; ISS-1839 (open, moderate). Self-applying.

patches.replace: Bradley (1728) first argued aberration was distinct from refraction; modern dispersion analysis confirms it.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon c5 H2 (minor): tldr_evidence over-attributes definitive proof to Bradley. The c4 detail_evidence softening did not propagate to the TLDR. Softening to 'first argued' aligns with the patched detail_evidence framing.

patches.replace: a pattern first argued by Bradley (1728) and confirmed by century-long spectroscopic and VLBI data.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon c5 H2 (minor): tldr_verdict still reads 'proven in 1728' after detail_verdict_text was softened in c4. Aligning TLDR with detail text framing.
